title: Fyers historical REST client
acceptance_criteria: Wraps the Fyers v3 history REST API (OHLCV candles per symbol per resolution). Responses typed via a dedicated interface (do NOT extend the WebSocket shim).
Reuses loadStoredToken(pool) from src/server/services/fyers-auth.ts for auth (read-only — do not modify that file).
Date-range chunking respecting Fyers per-request day caps per resolution; pagination across chunks.
Exponential backoff on HTTP 429; bounded max retries.

On HTTP 401 / auth failure mid-fetch: do NOT silently continue. Surface a typed resumable error indicating the last-successful cutoff so T-55 can checkpoint. Refresh-token use is best-effort ONLY if a non-null refresh_token is present — verify the real Fyers v3 flow; do not assume one exists.
Outbound requests target only the fixed Fyers host (no caller-supplied URLs — no SSRF surface).
Gated behind Fyers credential presence: with no creds, throw a clear loud error (never silent, never zero-data).
Fail loud on missing strikes / missing legs in a requested range (return explicit gap markers, never fabricate or zero-fill).
Records the adjusted-vs-unadjusted assumption explicitly in returned metadata.
Unit tests mock the HTTP layer: cover chunking math, 429 backoff, 401 resumable-error path, missing-strike gap marker, and no-creds loud failure. No live network calls.
Named exports only; TS strict; passes tsc --noEmit and lint.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
